meta.homeDescription: Microsoft Bookings with your contact, quarterly catalogue and clear eligibility for authorised Bacardi Belgium on-trade (Bacardi Limited).

supabaseEnv.setupLead: The portal is ready—you only need the project URL and anon key in `.env.local`. After saving, restart the dev server so Next.js picks up the variables.

issues.missing_anon_key: Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (Supabase → Project Settings → API → anon public).
issues.short_anon_key: The anon key looks truncated—paste the full string from the dashboard.

localeSwitcher.subtle: Same portal—pick how you read it.

brandShowcase.title: The family of brands behind BACARDÍ and Martini
brandShowcase.subtitle: A global portfolio of rum, gin, vodka, whisky, and liqueur—the same rigor on quality and compliance for Bacardi Belgium and authorised partners.

landing.pill: Quarterly catalogue · clear requirements · book after you choose
landing.heroP: Sign in to the portal, pick an activation from the catalogue and review requirements in real time. The Microsoft Bookings button appears on the activation detail page when you are eligible.
landing.previewDesc: Activation detail, requirement breakdown and eligibility-gated call to action.
landing.previewDisclaimer: Illustrative example of the portal experience.
landing.previewFigureAria: Illustrative preview: quarterly catalogue, requirement details and outlet eligibility indicator.
landing.stepsSubtitle: Flow for Belgian on-trade: sign in, choose an activation from the catalogue, then book on the detail page if eligible.
landing.step1d: Email, password or Google: open the quarterly catalogue and review requirements, evidence and eligibility status.
landing.step2d: Open the detail you care about: see the requirement breakdown and what you need to become eligible.
landing.step3d: When eligible, the Microsoft Bookings button on the detail page lets you book a slot with the contact.
landing.footerCopy: Workspace for authorised trade: quarterly catalogue, transparent eligibility and direct access to contacts. Operational support: Carda Bacardi Martini team.
landing.footerHeritage: Est. 1862 — authenticity, courage and integrity in how we work, from the Caribbean to your outlet.
landing.footerLegal: BACARDÍ, the bat device and other names shown are trademarks of Bacardi Limited Company or its affiliates. For authorised personnel and partners only.

login.title: Sign in to the activation portal
login.subtitle: Email and password, Google, or a link from your contact if your account is already enabled.
login.sentMagic: Check your inbox: we sent you an access link.
login.registered: Registration submitted. If Supabase requires email confirmation, open the link in your mail; otherwise you can sign in below.
login.authFail: Authentication failed. Try again or use email and password.
login.magicSummary: Sign in with email link (magic link)
login.magicHelp: If you received an invite or prefer passwordless access, enter your email below.

register.title: Create an account
register.subtitle: Email and password. In Supabase enable “Email” with password and, if needed, disable email confirmation in development.

config.title: Supabase configuration missing
config.p1: Public Supabase variables are not valid on the server (Node). Without them you cannot authenticate or read the database.
config.liAnon: — anon key from the Supabase dashboard
config.liSite: — public site URL (e.g. your Vercel domain), used for email links
config.deploy: After saving the variables, run a new
config.deployEnd: (or “Redeploy”) so Next.js picks them up in the build.
config.checksLi1: On Vercel, variables must be enabled for Production (not Preview only).
config.checksLi2: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL must be a valid URL, e.g. https://your-app.vercel.app (with https://).
config.checksLi3: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL should look like https://abcdefgh.supabase.co (no spaces).

error.title: Application error
error.p1: A server error occurred. In production, details are in Vercel logs (search for the digest below).
error.p2: Check: SQL migration applied on Supabase, env variables on Vercel, redirect URLs in Supabase Auth.

portalHome.bannerTitle: Book after you choose an activation
portalHome.bannerDesc: Open the detail page for the activation you want: when you are eligible, the Microsoft Bookings button is there.
portalHome.title: Activity catalogue
portalHome.intro: Here you find the current quarter’s activations. Eligibility is based on your profile: complete your details in
portalHome.loadErrorBody: Could not load activations. Check the Supabase connection and environment variables.
portalHome.emptyBody: No active catalogue for this period. Try again later or contact the Carda Bacardi team.

publicActivations.introGuest: Browse this quarter’s activations without an account. To calculate outlet eligibility and use Microsoft Bookings, sign in to the portal.

activation.eligibleP: You are eligible based on your profile data. Still complete the booking in Outlook to lock date and time with the team.
activation.bookingP: The portal records the booking intent and opens the public Microsoft Bookings page. Final confirmation happens in your Outlook calendar by completing the Bookings flow.
activation.bookingDefault: No dedicated URL is set for this activation: the
activation.guestRequirementsP: Sign in to the portal to evaluate requirements automatically from your outlet profile. Booking with the contact (Microsoft Bookings) is available on this same page after sign-in, when you are eligible.
activation.guestLoginToBook: Sign in to book on Bookings

booking.toastOk: Open Outlook to complete the booking.
booking.notEligible: You are not eligible for this activation. Update your profile or check the requirements.
booking.logFail: Could not record the click. Try again.

authCredentials.passwordMin: Password must be at least 6 characters.
authCredentials.supabaseMissingLogin: Supabase is not configured: add NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to .env.local in the project root, then restart `npm run dev`.
authCredentials.supabaseMissingRegister: Supabase is not configured: add NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to .env.local, then restart the dev server.
authCredentials.googleStartFailed: Could not start Google sign-in: check the provider in Supabase (Authentication → Providers → Google).
authCredentials.magicSupabaseEnv: Supabase is not configured. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to .env.local in the project root, then restart `npm run dev`.
